text: How does finding 1% help you scale to another percent? How is using a percent bra like using a rate table?

Did You Know?

When looking at the genes that cause different traits, like eye color, there are dominant and recessive versions.

For example, brown eyes are a dominant version and blue eyes are a recessive version of a gene. The two gene copies that we get from our biological parents determine our eye color. If we have one or two copies of the dominant brown eye gene, that will be our eye color, but it takes two copies of a recessive gene to see that trait. This might leave you thinking that is either one of your parents has brown eyes, you will also have brown eyes. While that is likely, people can carry recessive genes. If both of your parents have one blue eye recessive gene from your grandparents, then you could have the blue eyes gene passed down to you. The illustration shows how inherited dominant and recessive alleles can determine eye color.

Source: Lam, R., 2020. "What Are Dominant and Recessive Genes?" University of Minnesota, Genome Research Limited.
